target.title: fix(sanitize): preserve user-visible prose after error-prefix lines
target.body:
## Summary

When an assistant message stopped with `stopReason="error"` and the visible text started with an error prefix (e.g. `"Error:"`, `"request failed:"`), `sanitizeUserFacingText` replaced the whole payload with a templated error label. Any user-visible prose that followed the error line — recommendations, next steps, commands to try — was silently dropped from the channel reply.

- **Problem**: `sanitizeUserFacingText` error-context path rewrites the entire text when `ERROR_PREFIX_RE` matches, discarding multi-line prose that follows the error prefix
- **Solution**: Before rewriting, detect whether the text has multi-line content after the error prefix; if so, return only the non-prefix portion instead of a templated label
- **What changed**: `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` (+11 lines in the `errorContext` branch) + `sanitize-user-facing-text.test.ts` (new, 98 lines)
- **What did NOT change**: Single-line error fragments still flow into the existing rewrite logic unchanged; `errorContext: false` path is unaffected; no changes to callers or upstream text assembly

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #96007
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a Discord bot sends a multi-part reply and one section contains an inline tool error, users only see the error line — all subsequent recommendations and next steps are silently dropped. This makes it impossible for users to see the full agent response. The root cause is in `sanitizeUserFacingText`, which is channel-agnostic and affects all channel types, not just Discord.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Assistant reply text containing an error-prefix line followed by multi-line user-visible prose was replaced entirely with a templated error label, losing recommendations/next steps.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node 24.13.1, branch `fix/sanitize-error-context-96007`

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx -e '
import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js";

const text = `Error: connection timed out. fetch failed: SocketError: other side closed

Recommendation: try restarting the service and verify the config.
Next steps:
1. Check logs
2. Retry`;

const r = sanitizeUserFacingText(text, { errorContext: true });
console.log("Has Recommendation:", r.includes("Recommendation"));
console.log("Has Next steps:", r.includes("Next steps"));
console.log("Result:", r);
'

$ pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node --import tsx -e '...'
Has Recommendation: true
Has Next steps: true
Result: connection timed out. fetch failed: SocketError: other side closed

Recommendation: try restarting the service and verify the config.
Next steps:
1. Check logs
2. Retry

$ pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.test.ts --reporter=verbose
 ✓ preserves recommendations after an Error: timeout line
 ✓ preserves multi-paragraph content after a request-failed prefix
 ✓ preserves user-visible prose after a transport error prefix
 ✓ preserves guidance after an API Error prefix
 ✓ rewrites a bare Error: timeout line
 ✓ rewrites a bare transport error line (econnrefused)
 ✓ rewrites a transport error line without suffix content
 ✓ passes through full text when errorContext is false

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

**Before/after matrix**:

| Input | `errorContext` | Before fix | After fix |
|-------|---------------|-----------|----------|
| `Error: timeout\n\nReco: retry` | `true` | `LLM request timed out.` | `timeout\n\nReco: retry` |
| `Error: Connection refused\n\nTry again` | `true` | `LLM request failed: connection refused...` | `Connection refused\n\nTry again` |
| `request failed: conn reset\n\nSteps: 1. Check` | `true` | `LLM request failed: network connection was interrupted.` | `conn reset\n\nSteps: 1. Check` |
| `Error: timeout` (single line) | `true` | `LLM request timed out.` | `LLM request timed out.` (unchanged) |
| `Error: ECONNREFUSED` (single line) | `true` | `LLM request failed: connection refused...` | unchanged |
| `Error: timeout\n\nReco: retry` | `false` | Full text preserved | unchanged |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Multi-line text after an error-prefix line is preserved, with the error prefix stripped
  2. Single-line error fragments still produce the existing templated error labels
  3. Non-error context path (`errorContext: false`) is completely unaffected
  4. All 8 regression tests pass

- **What was not tested**: Live Discord roundtrip (requires a real Discord bot token and account); behavior on non-Linux platforms; interaction with `message_tool_only` source reply delivery mode

## Root Cause

In `sanitizeUserFacingText` (line 497), when `errorContext` is `true` and the trimmed text matches `ERROR_PREFIX_RE`, the function unconditionally rewrites the entire payload into a short templated error label via `formatTransportErrorCopy`, `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`, or `isTimeoutErrorMessage`. It does not check whether the text contains substantive multi-line prose after the error prefix that should be preserved.

The `errorContext: true` path is activated by `finalizeAssistantExtraction` in `embedded-agent-utils.ts:47-49` whenever `msg.stopReason === "error"`. An assistant message that generated user-visible prose but stopped with an error stop reason will have its entire visible text discarded.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Channel replies that contain an error-prefix line followed by user-visible prose (e.g. recommendations, next steps) will now show the prose content after the error prefix, rather than being replaced entirely by a short error label like "LLM request timed out."

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Multi-line error text preservation, single-line error rewrite, non-error-context passthrough
- **Edge cases checked**: Empty error prefix, empty suffix, text with only the error prefix and no content after
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live Discord delivery, multi-channel behavior (Telegram/Slack/WhatsApp), interaction with streaming/progress drafts

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The guard is placed at the exact rewrite point inside `sanitizeUserFacingText`, which is the single funnel for all user-facing error text. This is the narrowest boundary that fixes all channels at once.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The existing error-rewrite logic is well-structured; the only gap was the missing multi-line-prose detection.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding channel-specific guards in Discord's outbound adapter. Rejected because this is a text-sanitization problem, not a delivery problem — the fix should benefit all channels uniformly.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #96007 analysis, root-cause tracing through `sanitizeUserFacingText` → `finalizeAssistantExtraction` → `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads`, reproduction via `node --import tsx`, TDD with 8 test cases

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: The `ERROR_PREFIX_RE` is broad and could match user-authored prose that happens to start with "Error:" followed by multi-line content. In that case, the error prefix would be stripped.
- **Mitigation**: The guard only activates when there is substantive multi-line content after the prefix (checked via `includes("\n")`). The error prefix itself is the least useful part of the message — stripping it and keeping the rest is a net improvement over discarding everything.
- **Compatibility**: Fully backward-compatible. Single-line error fragments are unchanged. Non-error-context path is untouched. Callers do not need updates.

---

Fixes #96007
target.author: jiashu6
